findings.reason: 2 mandatory-domain control(s) missing/partial (includes a missing control)
no in-scope, in-date independent attestation (SOC 2 / ISO 27001) on file
1 critical / 4 high open; 2 SLA breach(es); oldest open 95d
material incident(s) affecting our data unresolved or disclosed late (> 3d)
subcontractor(s) process our data in an unapproved region or without evidence
missing/weak contractual security obligation(s): breach_notification_sla, right_to_audit
important/critical service with untested BCP or RTO worse than required
1 committed remediation item(s) past due and open

not_evaluable.why: control status unknown / no evidence supplied

considerations: Verify compensating controls (e.g., network segmentation, monitoring) not captured in the intake
Confirm attestation scope and exception dispositions with the supplier before adjudication
Check whether open items already have an accepted remediation plan owned by a named risk owner
Confirm data-classification and criticality ratings match the current engagement
Assess supplier concentration / substitutability separately (see handoffs)
disclaimer: Findings and evidence only; not a supplier approval, risk acceptance, or onboarding decision. A human risk owner must adjudicate. No system of record has been updated.
narrative: Third-party cyber risk review of supplier SUP-4471 (Vendor Alpha, coded) as of 2026-07-15 surfaced 8 evaluable findings spanning mandatory-control coverage, attestation currency, open critical vulnerabilities, an unresolved material incident affecting our data, fourth-party data exposure, contractual security terms, resilience, and remediation timeliness. Because this is a critical engagement handling regulated data, the deterministic mapping suggests a residual risk tier of Critical for human adjudication. The most material items are an open critical vulnerability past SLA and an incident affecting our data that remains open and was disclosed 22 days after it occurred. A human risk owner must weigh the cited evidence and any compensating controls and determine the disposition; this review provides findings and cited evidence only.
notes: Config version tpcr-cfg-2026.07. One control (BCP-1) was not evaluable and is listed separately; it is not treated as pass or fail.
